parameterGroups.label: Output (Inherited)
parameterGroups.rationale: Standard SoundGenerator base class parameters. Likely non-functional since this module produces no audio - it exists solely to host global modulators.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: Output volume as normalised linear gain. Inherited from SoundGenerator base - may have no effect since this module produces no audio.

parameters.name: Balance
parameters.description: Stereo balance. Inherited from SoundGenerator base - may have no effect.

parameters.name: Voice Limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of voices. Inherited from SoundGenerator base.

parameters.name: Kill Fade Time
parameters.description: Fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed. Inherited from SoundGenerator base.

disabledModChains.name: Pitch Modulation
disabledModChains.note: Disabled in base data. Standard SoundGenerator pitch chain - irrelevant for a non-audio container.

gaps.question: Does GlobalModulatorContainer produce any audio at all, or is renderNextBlock a no-op? How does it host modulators - are they in the 'Global Modulators' chain (chainIndex 1) or as child processors?
gaps.context: Critical for understanding whether the SoundGenerator base class parameters (Gain, Balance, VoiceLimit, KillFadeTime) are functional or vestigial.

gaps.question: How do child modulators get registered for global access? Is there a lookup mechanism that GlobalVoiceStartModulator/GlobalTimeVariantModulator/GlobalEnvelopeModulator use to find their source?
gaps.context: The producer/consumer relationship is the core architecture of the global modulation system - need to document how the connection works.

gaps.question: The 'Global Modulators' chain has constrainer '!Global*Modulator'. Does the '!' prefix mean 'exclude' these types? What modulator types CAN be added?
gaps.context: Users need to know which modulators can go in the container and which cannot (to avoid circular references).

gaps.question: Is the Gain parameter functional? Since this is a non-audio container, does the modMultiply composite (Gain * Global Modulators) actually do anything?
gaps.context: Need to determine if Gain/Balance/VoiceLimit/KillFadeTime are vestigial.

gaps.question: Does the GlobalModulatorContainer participate in voice allocation? It receives MIDI noteOn/noteOff as a SoundGenerator - does it allocate voices for the hosted modulators?
gaps.context: Per-voice modulators (EnvelopeModulator, VoiceStartModulator) hosted here need voice context to function.

gaps.question: Does the GlobalModulatorContainer have a custom editor or FloatingTile content type?

notes: GlobalModulatorContainer is the producer side of HISE's global modulation system. It is typed as a SoundGenerator but its purpose is to host modulators that can be referenced from anywhere in the module tree via GlobalVoiceStartModulator, GlobalTimeVariantModulator, GlobalEnvelopeModulator, and GlobalStaticTimeVariantModulator. The constrainer '!Global*Modulator' on the mod chain likely prevents adding consumer modules as children (which would be circular). The SoundGenerator base class parameters (Gain, Balance, VoiceLimit, KillFadeTime) may be vestigial - needs C++ verification. MatrixModulator also likely references modulators from this container.
